DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN OF DEMONSTRATING THAT PLAINTIFF DOES NOT POSSESS AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN EVIDENCE THAT HE WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY AND RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES PRECLUDE DEFENDANT FROM SUCCESSFULLY INVOKING THE FACTUALLY DEVOID PRONG OF AGUILAR. AN ORDER AFTER HEARING WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT AND AN ORDER ON RULING OF DEFENDANT'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS WAS SIGNED AFTER THE HEARING. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: PATRICIA RODRIGUEZ, CSR #12436

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT GEORGE F. SCHULER, INC.'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT GEORGE F. SCHULER, INC.'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS DENIED AS UNTIMELY PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 438(E). WHILE THAT SECTION DOES STATE THAT A LATE MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS MAY BE BROUGHT IF THE COURT PERMITS, THE COURT NEVER GRANTED AN APPLICATION FOR LEAVE TO FILE AN UNTIMELY MOTION. (SEE BURNETT V. CHIMNEY SWEEP (2004) 123 CAL.APP.4TH 1057, 1063.) PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION BASED ON TIMELINESS IS SUSTAINED. EVEN IF THE COURT DID CONSIDER THE MOTION, IT WOULD BE DENIED BECAUSE GROUNDS DO NOT APPEAR FROM THE FOUR CORNERS OF THE COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT ROBERTSHAW CONTROLS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: SANDY LEE OF AIKEN WELCH, CSR 	971

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE DRYWALL, INC.'S JOINDER IN DEFENDANT GEORGE F. SCHULER, INC.'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. NO ARGUMENT MADE AS TO THIS JOINDER'S MOTION AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE DRYWALL INC.'S JOINDER IN GEORGE F. SCHULER, INC.'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS DENIED AS UNTIMELY PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 438(E). WHILE THAT SECTION DOES STATE THAT A LATE MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS MAY BE BROUGHT IF THE COURT PERMITS, THE COURT NEVER GRANTED AN APPLICATION FOR LEAVE TO FILE AN UNTIMELY MOTION. (SEE BURNETT V. CHIMNEY SWEEP (2004) 123 CAL.APP.4TH 1057, 1063.) EVEN IF THE COURT DID CONSIDER THE MOTION, IT WOULD BE DENIED BECAUSE GROUNDS DO NOT APPEAR FROM THE FOUR CORNERS OF THE COMPLAINT. MOVING DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT ROBERTSHAW CONTROLS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S PROOF OF SERVICE SHOWS THAT IT PERSONALLY SERVED ITS JOINDER ON JULY 27, MAKING ITS JOINDER TIMELY. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: SANDY LEE OF AIKEN WELCH, CSR 	971

K. Albert Loquaci (415-890-5252) and Ethan H. Seibert (415-890-5184) of Nicolaides Fink Thorpe...LLP appearing for specially appearing and moving party United States Fire Insurance Company.
Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff and opposing party Eugene Millard.

RULING: SPECIALLY-APPEARING UNITED STATES FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFENDANT ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA
Parties stipulated to hear all matters simultaneously. Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Specially-Appearing United States Fire Insurance Company's Motion to Set Aside Default and Default Judgment Against Defendant Associated Insulation of California is denied. U.S. Fire lacks standing to bring this motion or appear in the action at this time. (W. Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal. App. 4th 1196, 1205 n. 12 ["Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 387, the trial court has discretion to permit a nonparty to intervene where the nonparty has a direct and immediate interest in the action"].) U.S. Fire has not demonstrated that it remains liable for any default judgment against Associated. (Id.) Moving party's request for Judicial Notice for Exhibits 2-11 is granted by the Court per Evidence Code 452(d). Court declines to take Judicial Notice of Exhibit 1. Order signed in chambers. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Miranda Rumsey, CSR # 14199, csrmirandar@gmail.com (Tel: 530-613-9821) (503/CML)

Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Williams Burrows, Inc.
Off Calendar Apr-30-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Donald Sullivan, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, CSR: Sonia Wong, #11288.

Oren Noah, Esq., of Brayton and Purcell appearing for Plaintiff, Eugne Millard.
Order Granting waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing on Application for Default Prove-up, on file.
No appearance by defendant(s) WILLIAMS BURROWS, INC.

The court stated on the record the following: In this personal injury asbestos case, the law allows joint and several liability for the economic damages sought.
However, for the non-economic damages sought, the law allows only several liability, i.e., each party or person is responsible for its, his or her own specific percentage of fault.
In these Requests to Enter Default Judgments, there is absolutely no evidence demonstrating the particular percentage of fault of any of these eight defendants in default.
The individual statements of damages are not helpful. They do not relate to any evidence of any amount of total non-economic damages nor any specific percentage of fault attributed to any defaulting defendant. The Statements of Damages merely act as a ceiling on the amount of non-economic damages that the court could award in this setting and little more.
Therefore, under CCP section 579, in my discretion, I do not find it to be proper to enter a several Default Judgment against any of these eight defaulting defendants. The eight motions or Requests for several Default Judgments are denied without prejudice and the court defers them to be heard at a later time by the trial court judge. It is my understanding that this matter is currently set for trial on December 19, 2013.

Off Calendar Aug-21-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
David A. Gifford of STEVENS, DRUMMOND & GIFFORD (925-944-5550), appearing for defendant / moving party George F. Schuler, Inc.
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Eugene Millard
Paul E. Hausknecht of HAKE LAW PC (415-364-0370), appearing for defendant / joinder Golden Gate Drywall, Inc.
Shelley K. Tinkoff of BRYDON HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant / joinder Perini Corp.
Ray Greene of BURNHAM BROWN (510-444-6800), appearing for defendant California Drywall Co.

RULING:
DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE DRYWALL, INC.'S JOINDER IN DEFENDANT GEORGE F. SCHULER, INC.'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS

No argument made as to this joinder's motion and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Golden Gate Drywall Inc.'s joinder in George F. Schuler, Inc.'s motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied as untimely pursuant to C.C.P. section 438(e). While that section does state that a late motion for judgment on the pleadings may be brought if the court permits, the court never granted an application for leave to file an untimely motion. (See Burnett v. Chimney Sweep (2004) 123 Cal.App.4th 1057, 1063.) Even if the court did consider the motion, it would be denied because grounds do not appear from the four corners of the complaint. Moving defendant's request for judicial notice of the order granting defendant Robertshaw Controls' motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant's proof of service shows that it personally served its joinder on July 27, making its joinder timely. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Sandy Lee of AIKEN WELCH, CSR 971 (503/TLJ)

RULING:
DEFENDANT GEORGE F. SCHULER, INC.'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant George F. Schuler, Inc.'s motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied as untimely pursuant to C.C.P. section 438(e). While that section does state that a late motion for judgment on the pleadings may be brought if the court permits, the court never granted an application for leave to file an untimely motion. (See Burnett v. Chimney Sweep (2004) 123 Cal.App.4th 1057, 1063.) Plaintiff's objection based on timeliness is sustained. Even if the court did consider the motion, it would be denied because grounds do not appear from the four corners of the complaint. Defendant's request for judicial notice of the order granting defendant Robertshaw Controls' motion for summary judgment is denied. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Sandy Lee of AIKEN WELCH, CSR 971 (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-12-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Michael J. Plocki of GORDON & REES (415-986-5900), appearing for defendant / moving party Rosendahl Corporation
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Eugene Millard

RULING:
DEFENDANT ROSENDAHL CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Rosendahl Corp.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is taken off calendar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Specifically, plaintiff's responses to defendant's special interrogatories, which were served prior to questioning by defendant's counsel at his deposition, stated that plaintiff has knowledge that the insulation materials and debris with which defendant worked contained asbestos and defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff does not have such knowledge. Plaintiff represents that he withdraws his strict liability and false representation causes of action and his claim for punitive damages, which were the only issues defendant sought to have summarily adjudicated. Defendant's motion was timely. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez, CSR #12436 (503/TLJ)

RULING:
DEFENDANT ALLIED PAINTERS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Allied Painters, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. RULING:
DEFENDANT REDWOOD PAINTING CO., INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Redwood Painting Co., Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting his claims against defendant. By failing to demonstrate with its moving papers that plaintiff submitted factually devoid responses to contention discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support his claims, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Amber R. Craig, Esq., of ARMSTRONG & ASSOCIATES, LLP (510-433-1830) appearing for defendant / moving party Exxon Mobil Corporation
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Eugene Millard

RULING:
DEFENDANT EXXON MOBIL CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Exxon Mobil Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied. As to the causation issue, Mr. Hagen's deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. As to duty, defendant's separate statement failed to properly make an issue of whether defendant owed a duty to plaintiff for affirmative acts of its employees. Even if defendant had properly made duty an issue, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that defendant owed no duty to plaintiff. SeaBright Ins. Co. v. US Airways, Inc. (2011) 52 Cal.4th 590 is inapplicable. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez, CSR #12436 = (503/TLJ)

Off Calendar Jun-14-2011 Text Ruling

APPEARANCES:
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / moving party Eugene Millard
James J. O'Brien, Esq., of JACKSON, JENKINS & RENSTROM, LLP (415-982-3600), appearing for defendant / opposing party Campbell Soup Company

RULING:
PLAINTIFF EUGENE MILLARD'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to compel the depositions of defendant Campbell Soup Company's person most knowledgeable and custodian of records and request for production of documents is denied. In light of the fact that defendant's PMK was recently deposed on some of the issues covered by the deposition notice and plaintiff's own deposition has not concluded, plaintiff has not provided good cause for its motion at the present time. Defendant's cross-motion for protective order is granted only to the extent that the motion seeks to defer the deposition sought by plaintiff until a reasonable time after the deposition of plaintiff has concluded. All requests for sanctions are denied. No party acted without substantial justification. Judge: Harold Kahn, Matter not reported = (220/HEK)
